name: Jimmy Witherspoon
abstract: James Witherspoon (August 8, 1920 – September 18, 1997) was an American jump blues and jazz singer.

interviews.title: Jimmy Witherspoon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, FL, April 12, 1996

concerts.label: Blues at Carnegie: Muddy Waters, Jimmy Witherspoon, Jimmy Reed, Oscar Brown, Jr., Big Maybelle
The Sensation of Music at Newport '61: Jon Hendricks' Evolution of the Blues
Midnight Performance: The Sensation of Music at Newport '61/ Jon Hendricks' Evolution of the Blues
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Blues in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: So we did a thing called 'Six Foot-Two Baby' or something with Spoon... and of course, we played for him.

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: Spoon had been with me before those other guys. But I guess I kept them together about a year.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: When we didn't have Jimmy Rushing we had Witherspoon, Jimmy Witherspoon.

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Longo
about_quotes.evidence: I wanted to ask you about comping behind Joe Williams and Nancy Wilson and Jimmy Witherspoon... And so I would listen to something the vocalist did and then play in between the phrases, and then we’d lock up together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Longo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Cotton
about_quotes.evidence: Ray Charles used to live down the street at the club on the Fosters... And Charles Brown, Jimmy Witherspoon, man, I've been on stage with a lot of people at the Dewdrop.

about_quotes.speaker: Don Smedley and Tom Stagg
about_quotes.evidence: they have visiting musicians, the type of Jimmy Witherspoon, and this type of singer does work with the Humphrey Littleton band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Smedley and Tom Stagg Interview, 1969-10-03
